_comment: SCOTUS Eval Gold Set — 10 hand-verified cases spanning all severity levels. Source: DB + CourtListener + SCOTUS opinion text + SCOTUSblog cross-reference. Created Session 1 (2026-02-24). Updated 2026-03-04 after full SCOTUSblog review.

cases.expected_winner: Dwayne Barrett / criminal defendants facing double convictions
cases.expected_loser: federal prosecutors seeking multiple convictions for single acts
cases.notes: 9-0 unanimous (Jackson authored). One act cannot yield two convictions under §924(c)(1)(A)(i) and §924(j). Clear people-win. Narrow statutory interpretation — tone should be measured, not dramatic. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.

cases.case_name: Department of Agriculture Rural Development Rural Housing Service v. Kirtz
cases.expected_winner: Reginald Kirtz / consumers with inaccurate credit reports
cases.expected_loser: federal agencies that report false credit information
cases.notes: 9-0 unanimous (Gorsuch authored). Consumers can sue federal agencies under FCRA. This is a PEOPLE-WIN case — tone should reflect a positive outcome for consumers, not doom. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.

cases.expected_winner: veterans like Simon Soto seeking combat-related compensation
cases.expected_loser: federal government seeking to limit retroactive benefit payments
cases.notes: 9-0 unanimous (Thomas authored). Veterans get CRSC without Barring Act cap. People-win case — tone should be measured and positive. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.

cases.expected_winner: IRS / federal government on estate tax valuation
cases.expected_loser: Connelly estate / closely-held business owners on tax planning
cases.notes: 9-0 unanimous (Thomas authored). IRS WON this case — corporation owes more taxes. GPT previously flipped winner/loser (said corp won, IRS lost). Technical estate tax ruling. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.

cases.case_name: Royal Canin U. S. A. v. Wullschleger
cases.expected_winner: Anastasia Wullschleger / consumers challenging deceptive marketing
cases.expected_loser: Royal Canin USA / corporations trying to keep cases in federal court
cases.notes: 9-0 unanimous (Kagan authored). Pure jurisdiction/procedure — federal court loses supplemental jurisdiction when federal claims are dropped, case goes to state court. Dry procedural ruling, tone should be matter-of-fact. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.

cases.expected_winner: Department of Veterans Affairs retaining discretion over claims
cases.expected_loser: veterans like Joshua Bufkin seeking disability benefits
cases.notes: 7-2 (Thomas authored). VA disability claims reviewed under deferential 'clear error' standard. Jackson + Gorsuch dissented. Issue is civil_rights (judicial review standard for veterans), NOT healthcare. GPT previously mislabeled issue_area as healthcare. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.

cases.expected_winner: federal government shielded by sovereign immunity
cases.expected_loser: bankruptcy trustees / creditors seeking to reclaim misappropriated funds
cases.notes: 8-1 (Jackson authored). Sovereign immunity waived for federal bankruptcy claims but NOT for state-law claims nested within them. Gorsuch dissented. Tone should be firm but not alarmist — GPT previously used 'tightens the grip of governmental power' which is overblown. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.

cases.expected_loser: TikTok users / free speech advocates / 170 million users
cases.notes: Per curiam (unsigned). TikTok divestiture law upheld as constitutional under intermediate scrutiny — content-neutral, targets foreign adversary control. No formal dissent — Sotomayor concurred in part, Gorsuch concurred in judgment. Major First Amendment ruling affecting 170M users. Level 4-5 appropriate. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.

cases.expected_winner: Trump / future candidates facing state-level disqualification
cases.expected_loser: Colorado voters / states seeking to enforce Section 3
cases.notes: Per curiam. 9-0 on outcome but deeply divided on reasoning. Only Congress (not states) can enforce Section 3 against federal candidates. Barrett concurred separately; Sotomayor/Kagan/Jackson concurred in judgment only — objected to majority reaching beyond what was necessary. NO dissent — GPT previously fabricated dissent_highlights for this case. Level 4-5 appropriate. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.

cases.expected_winner: state officials shielded from fee-shifting accountability
cases.expected_loser: civil rights plaintiffs who won preliminary injunctions but cannot recover fees
cases.notes: 7-2 (Roberts authored). Preliminary injunctions don't make you a 'prevailing party' for attorney's fees. Minor procedural ruling — impacts fee-shifting but not fundamental rights. Jackson + Sotomayor dissented. Tone should reflect this is a procedural setback, not a crisis. Verified via SCOTUSblog 2026-03-04.
